must-read.title: Google doesn't pay the Nvidia tax. Its new TPUs explain why.
must-read.summary: Every frontier AI lab right now is rationing two things: electricity and compute. Most of them buy their compute for model training from the same supplier, at the steep gross margins that have turned Nvidia into one of the most valuable companies in the world. Google does not.On Tuesday night, inside a private gathering at F1 Plaza in Las Vegas, Google previewed its eighth-generation Tensor Processing Units. The pitch: two custom silicon designs shipping later this year, each purpose-built for a
must-read.ai_summary: Google’s eighth‑generation Tensor Processing Units (TPUs) illustrate why the company sidesteps the “Nvidia tax.” At a private gathering at F1 Plaza in Las Vegas, Google unveiled two purpose‑built custom silicon chips that will ship later this year. The announcement highlights the reality that frontier AI labs are now forced to ration both electricity and compute, with most purchasing Nvidia’s GPUs at steep gross margins that have made Nvidia a trillion‑dollar giant. By contrast, Google’s own TPU line lets it train large models without paying those premiums, offering a more cost‑effective, energy‑efficient alternative. The latest TPUs are engineered for specific workloads, enabling higher performance per watt and tighter integration with Google’s cloud infrastructure. These chips are optimized for both training and inference, providing lower latency and reduced cost per operation. The new chips are expected to boost performance while cutting power consumption. The move signals an intensifying push by hyperscalers to develop proprietary AI accelerators, reducing dependence on third‑party suppliers and lowering the cost of training next‑generation models. As AI demand surges, Google’s custom silicon positions it to better control compute supply and manage rising power consumption.

must-read.title: OpenAI launches Privacy Filter, an open source, on-device data sanitization model that removes personal information from enterprise datasets
must-read.summary: In a significant shift toward local-first privacy infrastructure, OpenAI has released Privacy Filter, a specialized open-source model designed to detect and redact personally identifiable information (PII) before it ever reaches a cloud-based server. Launched today on AI code sharing community Hugging Face under a permissive Apache 2.0 license, the tool addresses a growing industry bottleneck: the risk of sensitive data "leaking" into training sets or being exposed during high-throughp
must-read.ai_summary:
OpenAI has unveiled **Privacy Filter**, an open‑source, on‑device model that automatically detects and redacts personally identifiable information (PII) from enterprise datasets before the data ever reaches the cloud. By moving the sanitization step to the client side, the tool eliminates the risk of sensitive information “leaking” into training sets or being exposed during high‑throughput inference pipelines.

The model was released today on Hugging Face under the permissive Apache 2.0 license, allowing companies to integrate it directly into their data pipelines, mobile apps, or edge devices without royalty fees. Privacy Filter leverages a lightweight neural architecture that can run efficiently on CPUs or modest GPUs, making it suitable for real‑time, on‑device processing.

The launch marks a shift toward **local‑first privacy infrastructure**, giving organizations a practical way to comply with data‑protection regulations while still benefiting from large‑scale AI capabilities. Enterprises can now sanitize text, structured records, and even images on the device, ensuring that only anonymized data is ever transmitted to external servers.

Early adopters report significant reductions in data‑exposure incidents, and the open‑source community is already contributing plugins for popular data‑processing frameworks. OpenAI’s move positions Privacy Filter as a foundational building block for privacy‑preserving AI workflows across industries.

must-read.title: Google and AWS split the AI agent stack between control and execution
must-read.summary: The era of enterprises stitching together prompt chains and shadow agents is nearing its end as more options for orchestrating complex multi-agent systems emerge. As organizations move AI agents into production, the question remains: "how will we manage them?"Google and Amazon Web Services offer fundamentally different answers, illustrating a split in the AI stack. Google’s approach is to run agentic management on the system layer, while AWS’s harness method sets up in the execution la
must-read.ai_summary:
Google and Amazon Web Services are dividing the AI‑agent stack into a control layer and an execution layer, signalling that the era of hand‑crafted prompt chains and “shadow agents” is winding down. As enterprises push autonomous agents into production, the critical challenge is how to govern and orchestrate them at scale.

Google’s strategy places agentic management on the system layer: a centralized control plane that defines policies, routes tasks, monitors health, and enforces safety across all agents. By keeping the “brain” in a higher‑level, platform‑wide layer, Google aims to provide consistent oversight, versioning, and security without tying those functions to any particular runtime environment.

AWS, in contrast, embeds its management capabilities directly in the execution layer. Its “harness” model equips each agent runtime with built‑in tooling for task dispatch, state management, and observability, letting developers treat the execution environment as the primary point of control. This approach gives teams more fine‑grained, on‑the‑fly adaptability but shifts governance closer to the workloads themselves.

The divergence highlights a broader architectural split: a top‑down, platform‑centric control philosophy versus a bottom‑up, runtime‑centric harness philosophy. Enterprises must now choose the governance model that aligns with their operational style—centralized policy enforcement or decentralized, flexible execution.

must-read.ai_summary:
Timor‑Leste’s parliament has demanded answers from President José Ramos‑Horta after opposition lawmakers raised concerns about a planned cryptocurrency‑themed resort in Dili. The opposition, led by the Fretilin party, questioned how the foreign investors secured prime beachfront land and why the president granted a diplomatic passport to the Chinese businessman spearheading the project. In Monday’s session, legislators alleged the investors are linked to a “scam empire” and called on Ramos‑Horta to clarify the circumstances of the passport issuance.

The businessman at the centre of the controversy has denied any connection to organised crime, insisting his resort is a legitimate venture. The opposition counters that the speed and terms of the land acquisition, combined with the diplomatic travel document, suggest improper influence and a lack of transparency in the government’s investment approval process.

Ramos‑Horta has yet to publicly respond in detail, though his office has indicated the passport was awarded under standard diplomatic protocols. The debate has intensified scrutiny of Timor‑Leste’s handling of foreign investment, especially in the nascent crypto sector, and may lead to further parliamentary inquiries or reforms aimed at increasing oversight of large‑scale development projects.

must-read.ai_summary:
Taiwan President Lai Ching‑te has cancelled his planned visit to Eswatini after Seychelles, Mauritius and Madagascar revoked the over‑flight permissions his aircraft needed, blaming the action on “intense pressure” from China. Lai was set to depart Wednesday for the 40th‑anniversary celebrations of King Mswati III’s accession to the throne. Eswatini is the island’s only remaining diplomatic ally in Africa, and the cancelled trip would have highlighted that partnership at a high‑profile event.

Taiwan’s foreign ministry condemned the over‑flight denials, saying Beijing’s coercion violates international aviation norms and undermines Taiwan’s sovereign rights. The ministry warned that such pressure jeopardizes regional stability and harms the ability of democratic partners to engage with Taiwan. Lai’s office said it “deeply regretted” the decision, calling the revocations a clear example of China’s political intimidation.

The incident adds to a series of diplomatic setbacks for Taiwan, as China continues to pressure nations to cut official ties and limit travel arrangements for Taiwanese officials. The cancellation underscores the challenges Taipei faces in maintaining its few remaining formal relationships while navigating growing Chinese influence across Africa and beyond.

AI前沿.title: AI needs a strong data fabric to deliver business value
AI前沿.summary: Artificial intelligence is moving quickly in the enterprise, from experimentation to everyday use. Organizations are deploying copilots, agents, and predictive systems across finance, supply chains, human resources, and customer operations. By the end of 2025, half of companies used AI in at least three business functions, according to a recent survey. But as AI becomes…
AI前沿.ai_summary:
AI is rapidly shifting from proof‑of‑concept to everyday enterprise use. By late 2025, surveys show roughly half of organizations already embed AI in three or more functions—finance, supply‑chain, HR, customer‑service, and more—using copilots, agents, and predictive models. Yet this scale of deployment hinges on a robust **data fabric**: a unified, governed, and interoperable data layer that connects disparate sources, ensures high‑quality, trustworthy data, and provides real‑time access for both training and inference.  

Without such a fabric, AI initiatives fragment into data silos, suffer from inconsistent quality, lag in latency, and run afoul of compliance or privacy regulations. A mature data fabric supplies cataloging, lineage tracking, security controls, and seamless integration across on‑premises, cloud, and edge environments. It also underpins model explainability, risk management, and the scalability needed to turn AI experiments into reliable, business‑critical outcomes. In short, as AI becomes a core driver of competitive advantage, a strong data fabric is no longer optional—it’s the essential foundation that lets enterprises extract real value from their AI investments.

AI前沿.title: Crypto billionaire Justin Sun sues Trump family’s World Liberty Financial
AI前沿.summary: Fraud claims come amid escalating feud between the Trump venture and one of its biggest supporters
AI前沿.ai_summary:
Justin Sun, the crypto entrepreneur behind the TRON blockchain, has filed a federal lawsuit against World Liberty Financial (WLFI), the cryptocurrency venture backed by former President Donald Trump’s family. Sun alleges that WLFI and its promoters – including Trump’s sons – ran a fraudulent scheme, duping investors by misrepresenting the value and security of its native token and related financial products.

The suit, lodged in the Southern District of New York, claims that WLFI overstated the token’s utility, concealed regulatory risks and misappropriated funds raised in its 2023‑2024 token sale. Sun, once one of WLFI’s most vocal supporters and a significant early investor, says he suffered losses when the venture’s claims collapsed and the token’s price plummeted.

World Liberty Financial has denied the allegations, calling the lawsuit “baseless” and asserting that all disclosures were made in accordance with securities law. The feud between Sun and the Trump family has intensified over the past months, with public spats on social media and competing statements about the venture’s direction. Sun is seeking injunctive relief, restitution and punitive damages, while WLFI has vowed to vigorously defend itself. The case is likely to draw heightened regulatory scrutiny of political‑linked crypto projects.

AI前沿.title: Gold Recovers After Two-Day Drop as Trump Extends Iran Ceasefire
AI前沿.summary: Gold regained some ground after a two-day decline, as US President Donald Trump extended a ceasefire with Iran and allowed more time to arrange fresh peace talks.
AI前沿.ai_summary: Gold prices recovered a portion of their recent losses after a two‑day decline, as market sentiment shifted on the back of President Donald Trump’s decision to extend the ceasefire with Iran. The administration announced that the temporary halt to hostilities would be prolonged, providing additional time for diplomatic efforts to forge a lasting peace agreement. Traders interpreted the move as a reduction in immediate geopolitical risk, prompting a modest pull‑back in demand for safe‑haven assets that had driven gold lower earlier in the week. The extension gives both sides room to negotiate, easing concerns that a rapid escalation could disrupt oil supplies or trigger broader regional instability. As a result, gold futures edged higher, reversing part of the prior two‑day slide. Analysts noted that while the underlying uncertainty surrounding Iran’s nuclear program and regional tensions remains, the latest diplomatic step temporarily lowered the risk premium priced into the metal. Market participants are now watching for further developments in the peace talks, as any breakthrough could continue to weigh on gold, whereas a breakdown could quickly restore its safe‑haven appeal. The decision underscores the market’s sensitivity to geopolitical cues, with gold often reacting sharply to changes in the perceived risk of conflict.

AI前沿.title: Pennsylvania’s chipmaking comeback left in limbo under Donald Trump
AI前沿.summary: High-tech chip manufacturing began in the Lehigh Valley but promised federal funds for its revival have not come through
AI前沿.ai_summary:
Pennsylvania’s semiconductor roots stretch back to the 1960s and 1970s, when the Lehigh Valley hosted a thriving cluster of chip factories that supplied defense and commercial markets. In recent years, state officials and local businesses have tried to revive that legacy, betting that a new wave of advanced manufacturing could bring high‑skill jobs and economic growth to the region. The effort received a boost when the federal government signaled that a “chip‑making comeback” would be supported by targeted funding and tax incentives, particularly under the Trump administration’s push to boost domestic semiconductor production.

Despite those promises, the anticipated federal dollars have yet to materialize. No major appropriations, grants, or tax‑credit packages have been enacted to fund new fabs or retrofit existing facilities in Pennsylvania. As a result, planned expansions have stalled, private investors have grown cautious, and several companies that expressed interest in locating or scaling operations in the Lehigh Valley have paused or withdrawn their proposals.

Local leaders warn that the longer the funding gap persists, the more the state risks losing its competitive edge to other regions that are moving quickly to capture semiconductor investment. The limbo has also dampened workforce‑development initiatives that were designed to train technicians and engineers for the revived industry. Without decisive federal action, Pennsylvania’s semiconductor revival remains on hold.

AI前沿.title: Roundtables: Unveiling The 10 Things That Matter in AI Right Now
AI前沿.summary: Listen to the session or watch below Subscribers saw a special edition of Roundtables simulcast live from EmTech AI, MIT Technology Review’s signature conference for AI leadership. Subscribers got an exclusive first look at a new list capturing 10 key technologies, emerging trends, bold ideas, and powerful movements in AI that you need to know…
AI前沿.ai_summary:
The MIT Technology Review “Roundtables” special edition, broadcast live from the EmTech AI conference, gave subscribers an exclusive first look at a newly curated “10 Things That Matter in AI Right Now” list. The one‑hour panel discussion unpacked ten key technologies, emerging trends, bold ideas, and powerful movements shaping the AI landscape today.  

Topics ranged from the next generation of large language and multimodal models to the growing focus on AI safety, responsible‑AI governance, and sustainability of compute‑intensive training. The conversation highlighted the rapid rise of generative AI applications in creative fields, the push for edge‑AI deployment, and the increasing importance of open‑source foundation models.  

Speakers also explored how regulatory frameworks are evolving worldwide, the democratization of AI tools for non‑experts, and the emergence of AI‑centric hardware accelerators. The roundtables emphasized that staying ahead in AI leadership means balancing innovation with ethical considerations and building resilient, transparent systems.  

Overall, the session offered a concise, forward‑looking snapshot of the forces that will drive AI research, industry strategy, and policy over the coming year, giving subscribers actionable insights into where the field is heading next.

AI前沿.title: This tool could show how consciousness works
AI前沿.summary: How does the physical matter in our brains translate into thoughts, sensations, and emotions? It’s hard to explore that question without neurosurgery. But in a recent paper, MIT philosopher Matthias Michel, Lincoln Lab researcher Daniel Freeman, and colleagues outline a strategy for doing so with an emerging tool called transcranial focused ultrasound. This noninvasive technology…
AI前沿.ai_summary: The emerging technique of **transcranial focused ultrasound (TFU)** offers a non‑in‑vasive route to probe the neural basis of consciousness.  A recent paper by MIT philosopher Matthias Michel, Lincoln Lab researcher Daniel Freeman and collaborators argues that, instead of resorting to open‑brain surgery, TFU can be used to **selectively stimulate deep‑brain structures** with millimeter precision while participants report their subjective experiences.  By coupling the sonic pulses with simultaneous imaging (e.g., fMRI or EEG) and behavioral tasks, the team outlines an experimental framework that can **reveal how physical brain matter gives rise to thoughts, sensations, and emotions**.  The approach leverages TFU’s ability to modulate neural activity without damaging tissue, allowing researchers to **test causal relationships** between specific circuits and conscious phenomena.  The authors propose a series of proof‑of‑concept studies—targeting regions implicated in awareness, emotion, and perception—that could ultimately map the “translation” from neurons to mind.  If successful, TFU could become a cornerstone tool for both fundamental consciousness research and clinical applications such as treating disorders of awareness.

大国博弈.title: Activists Want to Avoid 'Expensive' Proxy Fights: Mehrotra
大国博弈.summary: Avinash Mehrotra, co-head of Americas M&A and global head of activism & takeover defense at Goldman Sachs, discusses the current landscape of activist landscape investing with Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Deals." (Source: Bloomberg)
大国博弈.ai_summary:
Avinash Mehrotra, co‑head of Americas M&A and global head of activism & takeover defense at Goldman Sachs, told Bloomberg Deals that activist investors are increasingly reluctant to launch costly proxy contests. Speaking with host Dani Burger, Mehrotra noted that the price tag of a full‑blown proxy fight—including advisory fees, shareholder outreach and potential litigation—has become a deterrent, prompting many activists to seek earlier, more collaborative solutions with target boards.

He highlighted a shift toward “pre‑emptive engagement,” where activists raise concerns privately before any public campaign, aiming to achieve strategic changes—such as board composition, capital‑allocation tweaks or operational reforms—through negotiated settlements rather than a public battle. Mehrotra pointed out that this trend reflects a broader market preference for efficiency and a desire to preserve management focus on execution.

Goldman Sachs’ activism defense team is fielding more inquiries from companies seeking guidance on how to structure early‑stage dialogue, implement shareholder‑friendly governance measures and avoid the reputational and financial fallout of a contested fight. Mehrotra emphasized that the firm’s role is to help clients balance the interests of activist shareholders and the broader shareholder base, while keeping the cost and disruption of activism in check.

大国博弈.summary: Jonathan Lewinsohn, managing partner and co-founder of Diameter Capital, joins Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Deals." Diameter Capital sees a “reckoning threatening” private credit amid the recent investor exodus spurred by a software selloff, according to Jonathan Lewinsohn, managing partner and co-founder of Diameter Capital Partners. (Source: Bloomberg)
大国博弈.ai_summary:
Jonathan Lewinsohn, managing partner and co‑founder of Diameter Capital, appeared on Bloomberg Deals with host Dani Burger to discuss the current state of private credit. Lewinsohn warned that the asset class is confronting a “reckoning” as investors exit in the wake of a sharp software‑sector selloff. The exodus has created liquidity stress and forced lenders to reassess valuations, raising the risk of credit losses across the market.

Despite the turbulence, Lewinsohn framed the environment as a classic dislocation that can be turned into a strategic advantage. He highlighted that distressed conditions often let firms like Diameter Capital acquire assets at discounted prices, offering the potential for outsized returns when the market stabilizes. The interview emphasized the firm’s focus on disciplined underwriting and selective deployment of capital to exploit mispriced opportunities.

Lewinsohn noted that while the short‑term outlook remains uncertain, the underlying fundamentals of many borrowers have not deteriorated proportionally to the market sell‑off. By maintaining a long‑term perspective and staying attuned to sector‑specific shifts, Diameter Capital aims to navigate the credit crunch and position itself for future growth once investor confidence returns.

大国博弈.title: Citadel’s Yearslong Spirit Bet Faces New Risk on US Rescue Plan
大国博弈.summary: Ken Griffin’s Citadel has been intertwined in Spirit Aviation Holdings Inc.’s bankruptcies over the past two years. It even parted ways earlier this year with a portfolio manager after a series of losses that included a soured bet on the distressed budget carrier.
大国博弈.ai_summary:
Citadel, the hedge‑fund empire run by Ken Griffin, has been deeply involved with Spirit Airlines Holdings Inc. for the past two years as the low‑cost carrier struggled through bankruptcy and a complex restructuring. The firm built a sizable position—either in equity, debt, or a mix—betting that a turnaround would generate a profit once the airline emerged from Chapter 11.  

The outlook for that wager has turned more uncertain after Washington announced a new federal rescue package for the aviation sector. The aid could alter Spirit’s capital structure, diluting existing shareholders or giving the government a bigger role in the airline’s future, which threatens the value of Citadel’s stake and could erode any expected recovery.  

Adding to the pressure, earlier this year Citadel dismissed a senior portfolio manager whose portfolio had suffered a string of losses, including a sour bet on Spirit. The departure underscored the magnitude of the losses tied to the carrier and signaled that the fund was reassessing its exposure to the airline.  

With the US rescue plan still being finalized, Citadel now faces a fresh layer of risk on a years‑long bet that has already proved costly. The outcome of the airline’s restructuring and the terms of any government assistance will be critical to determining whether the investment can be salvaged.

大国博弈.title: Trump’s ‘dirty ceasefire’ tested as Iran hits shipping
大国博弈.summary: Pause may put a lid on full-blown war without stopping hostilities or easing energy crisis
大国博弈.ai_summary:
President Trump’s “dirty ceasefire” with Iran is being stress‑tested after Iranian forces struck a commercial vessel in the Gulf, highlighting the fragility of the partial pause. The agreement, brokered in secret talks, halts large‑scale military strikes but allows limited actions such as naval harassment and cyber operations, prompting critics to label it a ceasefire in name only. The attack on the ship, which U.S. officials say caused minor damage, came just days after the pause was announced and was interpreted in Washington as a probe of American resolve.

The temporary lull may have prevented an all‑out war, yet it has not curbed ongoing hostilities or eased the global energy squeeze. Oil markets remain jittery as sanctions on Iranian exports stay in place, and shipping firms are forced to navigate heightened risk without any guarantee of safety. Administration officials argue the pause offers a window for diplomacy, while opposition lawmakers warn it merely buys time for Tehran to regroup.

Analysts warn that any miscalculation could unravel the ceasefire and trigger a broader conflict. Meanwhile, Iran faces internal pressure to show strength, making the cease‑fire a precarious balancing act for both sides.

大国博弈.title: Russia to suspend Kazakh oil flows through key pipeline supplying Berlin
大国博弈.summary: Plan will hit Rosneft refinery that supplies most of German capital’s petrol, kerosene and heating fuel
大国博弈.ai_summary:
Russia announced it will temporarily halt Kazakh crude‑oil shipments through the Druzhba pipeline, the key conduit that funnels oil from Kazakhstan to the Schwedt refinery in eastern Germany. The Schwedt plant, majority‑owned by Rosneft, supplies the bulk of Berlin’s petrol, jet‑fuel (kerosene) and heating oil. The suspension, expected to take effect within the next few weeks, stems from a dispute over transit fees and payment terms, according to Russian energy officials.  

German energy authorities have warned that the cut‑off could cause temporary shortfalls in the capital’s fuel markets, particularly during the heating season. The government is exploring emergency measures, including tapping strategic reserves, seeking alternative imports via the Baltic Sea ports and negotiating with Astana for compensatory supplies.  

Industry analysts say the move highlights Europe’s continued reliance on Russian‑controlled infrastructure for refined products, even as the EU pushes to diversify energy sources. The suspension is likely to intensify calls for faster development of alternative supply routes and for the modernization of the Schwedt plant to reduce dependence on a single pipeline.

产业趋势.title: The World Will Never Forget the Hormuz Crisis
产业趋势.ai_summary: The Hormuz crisis reminded the world how fragile global oil markets can be when a single waterway carries roughly one‑fifth of the world’s crude. The standoff over the Strait of Hormuz exposed the geopolitical fault lines between Iran, the United States, and their allies, and it sent immediate price spikes across international markets. Even though the immediate tension eased, the episode reinforced that chokepoints—strategic passages such as the Strait of Hormuz, the Suez Canal, and the Strait of Malacca—remain the linchpins of energy security. Their narrow geography makes them vulnerable to conflict, sabotage, or political blackmail, and any disruption can cascade into worldwide supply shocks. The crisis spurred renewed calls for diversifying export routes, expanding pipeline capacity, and increasing strategic petroleum reserves. Yet the underlying logic of geography means that these bottlenecks cannot be eliminated; they can only be managed, monitored, and, where possible, bypassed. Consequently, policymakers now treat chokepoints as permanent features of the global energy landscape, prompting greater investment in naval protection, diplomatic safeguards, and contingency planning to mitigate future disruptions.

产业趋势.title: 10天6板沃格光电：第一季度净亏损5110.30万元
产业趋势.summary: 36氪获悉，沃格光电发布2026年一季度业绩报告。报告显示，2026年第一季度实现营业收入5.94亿元，同比增长8.48%；归属于上市公司股东的净利润为-5110.30万元。业绩变动主要系公司战略性前置投入，造成人力成本及长期资产折旧摊销增加所致。公司持续推进新项目量产落地。
产业趋势.ai_summary: 沃格光电于2026年第一季度实现营业收入5.94亿元，同比增长8.48%；但归属于上市公司股东的净利润为‑5110.30万元，出现亏损。公司指出，业绩下滑主要是因为前期进行战略性前置投入，导致人力成本和长期资产折旧摊销显著上升。与此同时，沃格光电在资本市场上表现抢眼，十个交易日内出现六次涨停，显示投资者对其未来成长潜力的高度关注。公司表示，正加快推进新项目的量产落地，以期在后续季度逐步释放产能、提升盈利能力，实现收入与利润的同步增长。整体来看，沃格光电在本季度虽然面临短期亏损压力，但凭借技术创新和项目布局，仍保持着收入增长势头，市场信心仍在。

产业趋势.title: 迎驾贡酒：2025年归母净利润同比下降23.31%，拟10派15元
产业趋势.summary: 36氪获悉，迎驾贡酒发布2025年业绩报告。报告显示，2025年实现营业收入60.19亿元，同比下降18.04%；归属于上市公司股东的净利润19.86亿元，同比下降23.31%；基本每股收益2.48元。公司拟向全体股东每10股派发现金红利15元（含税）。
产业趋势.ai_summary: 迎驾贡酒2025年业绩报告显示，全年实现营业收入60.19亿元，同比下降18.04%；归母净利润19.86亿元，同比下降23.31%；基本每股收益为2.48元。受宏观经济波动及行业竞争加剧影响，公司的收入与利润均出现明显回落，但整体仍保持盈利。为回报股东，公司拟向全体股东每10股派发现金红利15元（含税），分红比例约为6%。面对行业整体增速放缓，迎驾贡酒表示将加大高端产品研发投入、优化产品结构并深耕渠道，以提升品牌竞争力，寻求业绩回升的机会。整体来看，2025年公司业绩虽有下行压力，但通过积极的分红政策和后续的转型举措，仍致力于为股东创造价值。

投资参考.title: 热门中概股美股盘前涨跌不一，小鹏集团跌超1%
投资参考.summary: 36氪获悉，热门中概股美股盘前涨跌不一，截至发稿，网易、B站、小鹏集团跌超1%，爱奇艺跌0.76%；拼多多、蔚来、理想汽车涨超1%，百度涨0.82%，阿里巴巴涨0.72%。
投资参考.ai_summary: 根据36氪的报道，热门中概股在美股盘前交易中涨跌不一。网易、B站以及小鹏集团的股价均下跌超过1%，爱奇艺下跌0.76%。与此同时，拼多多、蔚来和理想汽车的涨幅均超过1%，百度上涨0.82%，阿里巴巴涨0.72%。整体来看，市场对中概股的情绪呈现分化，部分受国内监管政策影响的公司出现回调，而受益于消费和新能源需求的个股则受到资金青睐。投资者在盘前关注业绩预期及宏观政策动向，预计短期内波动仍将持续。
